config.rationale: First experiment (0/30) — establishing an SGLang baseline for Qwen3.5-9B on a single H100 80GB before testing MTP/NEXTN. Per rules, baselines must come first with no quantization, no speculative decoding, kv_cache_dtype=auto, fcfs scheduling. The user wants to measure the speedup from MTP (NEXTN) — that requires a clean reference run without it. Choosing SGLang because the user's MTP request targets SGLang specifically; the next experiment will turn on --speculative-algo NEXTN with the same other settings so the delta is purely attributable to MTP. Sizing: model is ~7.46B params (~15 GB in bf16), H100 has 80 GB, leaving ~65 GB for KV cache and activations. max_model_len=65536 is a safe starting point (binary-search style); we can double to 131072 in a later run if VRAM permits. mem_fraction_static=0.85 is SGLang's standard balanced default for an 80GB card. TP=1 since only 1 GPU. No prefix caching / no chunked prefill yet — keep baseline minimal so MTP comparison is fair.

llm_commentary: Baseline SGLang run for Qwen3.5-9B on a single H100 establishes solid reference numbers: 7156 tok/s peak throughput and a very healthy 155.8 ms TTFT p95, with all smoke tests (chat, tool calling, JSON mode/schema) passing. As the only completed run, it trivially holds the top spot across all three goals — best_throughput, best_latency, and best_balanced — though "partial" status indicates some metrics may still be incomplete. With 29 experiments remaining and the user explicitly interested in MTP/NEXTN speedups, this is a clean baseline to measure deltas against.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-sglang-4eb71ad47b0b --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d lmsysorg/sglang:latest python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tp-size 1 --dtype auto --enable-metrics --mem-fraction-static 0.85 --context-length 65536 --disable-radix-cache --reasoning-parser qwen3 --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder

post_benchmark_correctness.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)
